name: deno_core
description: V8-based JavaScript runtime engine that powers Deno, providing high-performance JS execution with Tokio async integration

implementation.comment: Rust bindings to the V8 JavaScript engine via rusty_v8

first-release.comment: First published to crates.io September 2019; Deno project started 2018

typing.comment: JavaScript is dynamically typed; TypeScript requires transpilation

paradigm.comment: Supports all JavaScript paradigms via V8

pattern-matching.comment: JavaScript has destructuring but no true pattern matching

async-await.comment: Full async/await support via V8; integrated with Tokio event loop

iterators-generators.comment: Supports ES6 iterators, generators, and for-of loops

module-system.comment: Supports ES modules; module resolution customizable via ModuleLoader trait

standard-library.comment: Bare-bones V8 context; no console, fetch, or I/O by default. Must add via extensions.

execution-strategy.comment: V8's TurboFan JIT compiler and Sparkplug baseline compiler

compilation-model.comment: Runtime eval and snapshot support for precompiled startup

hot-reloading.comment: Scripts can be re-evaluated at runtime via execute_script

repl-support.comment: Can evaluate code interactively; Deno CLI provides full REPL

debugging-support.comment: V8 Inspector protocol support for Chrome DevTools debugging

startup-time.comment: V8 initialization is heavy (>100ms); snapshots can reduce cold start

execution-speed.comment: V8 JIT compilation provides excellent runtime performance

memory-overhead.comment: V8 isolate requires significant memory (>50MB baseline)

garbage-collection.comment: V8 uses Orinoco generational garbage collector

pure-rust.comment: Depends on rusty_v8 which wraps V8 C++ engine

api-safety.comment: Safe public API wrapping V8; unsafe contained in rusty_v8 bindings

idiomatic-api.comment: Good macro support (#[op2]), serde integration, Result-based errors, but steep learning curve

serde-integration.comment: serde_v8 for serialization; FromV8/ToV8 derive macros

async-compatible.comment: Built on Tokio; async ops map JavaScript Promises to Rust Futures

function-registration.comment: #[op2] macro and extension! macro for registering Rust functions

type-mapping.comment: FromV8 and ToV8 traits with derive macros; serde_v8 for conversions

blank-slate-mode.comment: Starts with minimal V8 context; no console, timers, or I/O by default

memory-sandboxing.comment: V8 isolates provide memory isolation between contexts

cpu-limits.comment: No built-in instruction counting; would need external timeout mechanism

memory-limits.comment: V8 heap size limits can be configured via RuntimeOptions

syscall-restrictions.comment: No I/O capabilities by default; all access must be explicitly added via ops

capability-security.comment: deno_core itself has no capability system; Deno CLI adds permissions layer

default-deny.comment: Minimal runtime with no I/O by default; must explicitly grant capabilities

share-data.comment: serde_v8 supports zero-copy for buffers and strings where possible

custom-types.comment: Can expose Rust types via ops and serde_v8

callbacks-events.comment: JavaScript functions can be stored and called as callbacks

github-stars.comment: deno_core repo; main deno repo has 100k+ stars

package-manager.comment: No built-in package manager; can integrate with npm/deno registries

editor-support.comment: Uses standard JavaScript/TypeScript tooling; Deno LSP available

example-projects.comment: Powers Deno CLI; used for custom JS/TS runtimes and plugin systems

maintenance-status.comment: Very active development; 342 releases, updated daily

documentation-quality.comment: 30% documented on docs.rs; good official tutorials but thin API docs

maturity.comment: Powers production Deno runtime; battle-tested at scale

primary-use-cases.comment: Custom JavaScript runtimes, plugin systems, automation tools
